summary: Started decompilation of the mnvibration menu module, replacing many placeholder prototypes with typed signatures, adding local data/asset structs, JObj helper inlines, globals/static data, and substantial C implementations for controller rumble toggling, name-list scrolling, cursor movement, text cleanup, and menu object setup. The PR body explicitly called this WIP with preliminary comments to be validated in follow-up PRs. The decomp.dev report showed 5 new full matches and 12 partial improvements in main/melee/mn/mnvibration, for +1132 matched bytes and +0.03% overall matched code.
what_changed: Expanded src/melee/mn/mnvibration.c from a mostly placeholder module into a large partial implementation, adding 944 lines and deleting 6.

Added includes for ft_0C31, gm_1A36, gmmain_lib, lb_00B0, lb_00F9, lbarchive, lbaudio_ax, mn/inlines, mnmain, mnname, mn/types, Dolphin OS, and several baselib headers needed by the reconstructed menu code.

Added local inline JObj translation getters/setters named mnVibration_JObjGetTranslationX/Y/Z and mnVibration_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z, including assert calls and the ftCo_800C6AFC invalidation/update pattern when JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT is not set.
Introduced static/global data used by the module, including mnVibration_804D4FE8, mnVibration_804D4FF0, mnVibration_804D6C28, mnVibration_803EECEC, and mnVibration_803EECF8.

Added local structs MnVibrationJointAssets and MnVibrationData, extending the existing MnVibrationAssets usage with documented offsets for controller state bytes, scroll offset, JObj arrays, text handles, title text, and cursor GObj.

Implemented or partially implemented functions around the vibration menu: mnVibration_802474C4, fn_80247510, fn_80248084, mnVibration_802480B4, mnVibration_8024829C, mnVibration_80248444, mnVibration_80248644, fn_80248748, fn_802487A8, fn_80248A78, and mnVibration_80248ED4.

Reworked header prototypes in src/melee/mn/mnvibration.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS placeholders to typed declarations using HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, u8, s32, and return types such as void, s32, and HSD_JObj*.

Added MnVibrationFloatData to the header with pad0[0x18], x18, and x1C fields, plus externs for mnVibration_803EECE0, mnVibration_804DC020, mnVibration_803EED04, mnVibration_804D4FF4, and mnVibration_804D4FFC.

Menu behavior reconstructed includes B-button exit cleanup, A-button rumble toggle per controller, L-held name-list navigation, persistent name rumble flag toggling via GetPersistentNameData(...)->x1A1, text creation/destruction, JObj child creation/removal, and pad rumble add/remove calls.
smart_moves: Kept JObj translation access as module-local inlines rather than blindly using generic helpers, allowing the generated assert line numbers and ftCo_800C6AFC update pattern to match this module's codegen constraints.

Modeled the menu's user_data as MnVibrationData with explicit offsets, which made repeated accesses to controller state, scroll_offset, jobjs, texts, title_text, and cursor_gobj more readable while preserving matching-sensitive layout.
Separated repeated asset tuples into MnVibrationJointAssets for joint, animjoint, matanim, and shapeanim, matching the recurring HSD_JObjLoadJoint/HSD_JObjAddAnimAll pattern.

Used small static frame/animation data structures such as AnimLoopSettings mnVibration_803EECEC and f32 mnVibration_803EECF8 to expose hidden data dependencies instead of leaving magic pointers opaque.
Converted only evidence-supported prototypes in the header while leaving remaining address-based symbol names where semantic names were not yet validated.
PR intentionally labeled the comments/documentation as preliminary and WIP, reducing the risk of overclaiming semantics before later validation.
decomp_lessons: For menu modules, recovering the GObj user_data struct is often the central unlock: once arrays of JObjs and HSD_Text handles are laid out, input handling, cursor animation, and cleanup become much easier to express.

When matching code that manipulates JObj translation fields directly, preserve assert strings/line numbers and the matrix-update side effect; generic helper calls may not match if the original was an inline pattern.

Controller/menu code may require joining multiple subsystems in one translation unit: gm input access, gmMainLib rumble settings, HSD_PadRumble APIs, lbAudioAx sound effects, mn name APIs, sislib text, and JObj animation all interact here.

Name-list UI reconstruction should handle both visible row and scroll offset separately; this PR repeatedly computes name_idx from scroll_offset + cursor_row and uses 0xFF as an invalid sentinel when the row is beyond GetNameCount().
Text cleanup matters for matching and correctness: this module explicitly frees each of 8 HSD_Text handles and title_text with HSD_SisLib_803A5CC4 before unlinking the menu GObj.
A partially matched WIP PR can still be valuable if it lands typed prototypes, local structs, and matched leaf/helper functions that future PRs can build on.
naming_conventions: Module symbols use the established mnVibration_802xxxxx address-based naming when semantics are not yet known.
File-local helper inlines use a more descriptive module-prefixed CamelCase style: mnVibration_JObjGetTranslationX/Y/Z and mnVibration_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z.
Anonymous or not-yet-understood callbacks remain fn_802xxxxx, such as fn_80247510, fn_80248084, fn_80248748, fn_802487A8, and fn_80248A78.
Recovered structs use descriptive PascalCase names with module prefix: MnVibrationAssets, MnVibrationJointAssets, MnVibrationData, and MnVibrationFloatData.
Unknown struct fields retain offset-style names such as x0, x6, x18, x1C, and pad_0B when semantics are incomplete.
Static/global data retains address-based names such as mnVibration_804D4FE8, mnVibration_804D4FF0, mnVibration_804D6C28, mnVibration_803EECEC, and mnVibration_803EECF8.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used local static inline JObj getters/setters with explicit __assert file/line constants, direct translate field loads/stores, and ftCo_800C6AFC calls to reproduce expected codegen.
Preserved signed/unsigned casts around controller indices and pad status checks, for example casting HSD_PadCopyStatus[(u8)i].err through u8 and s8.
Used explicit u8 fields for controller states, cursor row, scroll offset, connected flags, and rumble settings to control loads/stores and comparisons.
Kept repeated child traversal code explicit rather than abstracting it away, matching patterns that walk jobj->child and jobj->next with NULL checks.
Introduced PAD_STACK markers in functions where stack shape needed adjustment, including mnVibration_80248444 and fn_80248748.
Replaced a function-pointer-style call to mnVibration_80248ED4 with a direct typed call using saved_arg after the prototype was recovered.

Relied on decomp.dev feedback showing full matches for mnVibration_802480B4, mnVibration_8024829C, fn_80248748, mnVibration_802474C4, and fn_80248084, with large partial percentages for several remaining functions.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The PR body says: 'still WIP' and notes that preliminary documentation/comments would be more strongly validated in follow-up PRs.
The decomp.dev bot reported +1132 matched bytes, +0.03% overall matched code, 5 new full matches, and 12 improvements in unmatched items for main/melee/mn/mnvibration.
follow_up_queries: Find follow-up PRs that further validate or rename mnvibration comments and symbols introduced as WIP in PR 2142.
Search for later changes to MnVibrationData field names, especially x0, x6, scroll_offset, jobjs, texts, title_text, and cursor_gobj.
Search for later full matches of fn_80247510, fn_802487A8, fn_80248A78, mnVibration_80248444, mnVibration_80248644, and mnVibration_80248ED4.
Compare JObj translation inline patterns in other modules that use ftCo_800C6AFC and assert line constants.
Check whether mnVibration_804D4FE8's controller-to-JObj index mapping {0x16,0x15,0x14,0x13} receives a semantic name later.
